title: API Gateway Mastery Challenge
description: 20 realistic scenario-based questions focusing on API Gateway design, security, integration, and performance.

questions.questionText: Your team is creating a REST API using API Gateway and Lambda. You want to protect endpoints from being overwhelmed by high-frequency calls. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Usage plans and throttling protect APIs by limiting call frequency per API key.

questions.questionText: A developer needs to ensure all incoming requests to API Gateway come through HTTPS only. What setting should they enforce?
options: C) Set up an AWS WAF rule
D) Require TLS in the endpoint configuration
questions.explanation: API Gateway requires HTTPS for secure communication; this is enforced in the endpoint settings.

questions.questionText: Your backend Lambda returns HTTP status codes in the response body, but your API Gateway endpoint always returns 200. How can you fix this?
options: A) Enable CORS on API Gateway
questions.explanation: Lambda proxy integration forwards full HTTP responses from Lambda to the client.

questions.questionText: Your team is using API Gateway with a VPC link to access services in a private subnet. The API Gateway endpoint is returning 504 errors. What is the likely cause?
options: A) The VPC endpoint is not associated with a subnet
B) The Lambda function timed out
C) The Network Load Balancer is not configured correctly
D) API Gateway does not support VPC links
questions.explanation: 504 errors usually indicate the NLB is not responding properly to the VPC link traffic.

questions.questionText: You are required to validate incoming JSON payloads for an API. What is the best way to enforce schema validation at the API Gateway level?
options: A) Use Lambda to validate requests
B) Add AWS WAF with regex rules
C) Configure request models with JSON schema and enable validation
questions.explanation: API Gateway allows JSON schema validation by defining models and enabling request validation.

questions.questionText: You're using API Gateway to trigger a Lambda function, but you need custom response formatting before sending it to the client. What should you use? (Scenario ID: 6)
options: B) Integration response with mapping templates
questions.explanation: Mapping templates allow customization of the integration response to the client.

questions.questionText: A mobile app consumes your API Gateway endpoint. You need to enable third-party web clients to access it. What should you configure? (Scenario ID: 7)
options: A) Enable CORS in API Gateway
questions.explanation: CORS is required for cross-origin browser access to APIs.

questions.questionText: You're using API Gateway to trigger a Lambda function, but you need custom response formatting before sending it to the client. What should you use? (Scenario ID: 8)
options: B) Integration response with mapping templates
questions.explanation: Mapping templates allow customization of the integration response to the client.

questions.questionText: A mobile app consumes your API Gateway endpoint. You need to enable third-party web clients to access it. What should you configure? (Scenario ID: 9)
options: A) Enable CORS in API Gateway
questions.explanation: CORS is required for cross-origin browser access to APIs.

questions.questionText: You're using API Gateway to trigger a Lambda function, but you need custom response formatting before sending it to the client. What should you use? (Scenario ID: 10)
options: B) Integration response with mapping templates
questions.explanation: Mapping templates allow customization of the integration response to the client.

questions.questionText: Your company wants to monitor API key usage across multiple clients. Which feature of API Gateway helps? (Scenario ID: 11)
options: B) Usage plans and API keys
questions.explanation: API keys linked to usage plans let you monitor and throttle individual client usage.

questions.questionText: You want to log the full HTTP request and response from API Gateway to CloudWatch for debugging. What should you enable? (Scenario ID: 12)
options: A) Enable execution logging in stage settings
questions.explanation: Execution logging captures request and response metadata in CloudWatch logs.

options: A) Enable CORS in API Gateway
questions.explanation: CORS is required for cross-origin browser access to APIs.

questions.questionText: A developer wants to pass environment-specific variables (like stage name) to the backend from API Gateway. What should they use? (Scenario ID: 17)
questions.explanation: Stage variables let you inject config values into integration requests or headers.

rewards.description: Earn 1500 XP for demonstrating API Gateway expertise!
